# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Application Overview
Beudox HR is a comprehensive Human Resources Management System built as a single-page React application. It serves as a complete HR solution for organizations, managing employees, evaluations, projects, clients, invoices, policies, and loans through a modern web interface.

## Architecture & Technology Stack

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type-safe component development
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation and HMR
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes and role-based navigation
- **State Management**: React Query (TanStack Query) for server state, React Context for auth state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens, shadcn/ui component system
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod validation schemas
- **UI Components**: Radix UI primitives via shadcn/ui (40+ components)
- **Icons**: Lucide React icon library
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap editor for policy documents

### Backend Architecture
- **Platform**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Edge Functions + Storage)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and magic links
- **Real-time**: Supabase real-time subscriptions for live updates
- **APIs**: RESTful endpoints via Supabase client, custom Edge Functions
- **File Storage**: Supabase Storage for avatars, documents, and attachments

### Development & Testing
- **Linting**: ESLint with React and TypeScript rules
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests
- **Type Checking**: TypeScript with strict mode
- **Code Quality**: Prettier for formatting, Husky for git hooks

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Authentication Flow
- Supabase Auth manages sessions with `onAuthStateChange` listener
- Auth context provides user, employee, role, and company data app-wide
- Protected routes check authentication and role permissions
- Password reset flow uses Supabase's built-in email system

### Data Fetching Strategy
- React Query manages all server state with caching and background updates
- Queries are keyed by resource type and filters for optimal caching
- Mutations invalidate related queries to maintain consistency
- Optimistic updates for better UX on form submissions

### Component Organization
- Feature-based folder structure under `src/components/`
- Shared UI components in `src/components/ui/` (shadcn/ui)
- Layout components handle responsive design and navigation
- Custom hooks encapsulate business logic and data fetching

### Role-Based Access Control
- Permission checking via `canAccess(role, pathname)` function
- UI elements conditionally render based on user role
- Database RLS policies enforce data-level security
- Navigation items filtered by role permissions

## Database Schema Overview

### Core Entities
- **companies**: Organization information and settings
- **employees**: User profiles with auth integration
- **roles**: Permission levels (employee, team_lead, hr_manager, ceo)
- **departments**: Organizational structure

### HR Features
- **evaluations**: Quarterly performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback system
- **evaluation_parameters**: Customizable rating criteria
- **hr_policies**: Company policies and documents

### Business Features
- **projects**: Project management with team assignments
- **clients**: Client relationship management
- **invoices**: Billing and invoicing system
- **loans**: Employee loan tracking

### Supporting Tables
- **public_holidays**: Company holiday calendar
- **employee_roles**: Many-to-many role assignments
- **project_assignments**: Project team member relationships

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Application Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: React root rendering with strict mode
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing and providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Legacy entry point (may be deprecated)

### Authentication System
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Auth context and session management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Authentication form
- `src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx`: Password reset initiation
- `src/pages/SetPassword.tsx`: Password update form

### Layout & Navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main app shell
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page header with breadcrumbs
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Styled navigation link wrapper

### Core Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variants
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection dropdown
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Evaluation history display
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx`: Rich text editing for policies

### Business Logic
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/format-role.ts`: Role formatting helpers

### Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration with path aliases
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS customization
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `eslint.config.js`: Linting rules
- `playwright.config.ts`: E2E testing configuration

## Data Flow & Business Logic

### Employee Onboarding Flow
1. User invited via email (Supabase Auth invite)
2. User sets password and completes profile
3. Employee record created with role assignment
4. User gains access based on role permissions

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with ratings and recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback system with directional ratings
- **Visibility Rules**: Role-based access to evaluation data
- **Timeline View**: Chronological display of all evaluations for an employee

### Project Management
- Projects have clients, team members, and status tracking
- Invoices generated from project data
- Time tracking and billing integration
- Role-based access to project information

### Invoice Generation
- PDF generation via Supabase Edge Functions
- Email delivery with attachment support
- Client information and payment tracking
- Integration with project management

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
- `npm run dev`: Starts Vite dev server on port 8080
- Hot module replacement for instant updates
- Component tagging in development for debugging

### Building & Deployment
- `npm run build`: Production build with optimization
- Static assets served from `dist/` directory
- Environment variables prefixed with `VITE_` exposed to client

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for component behavior
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows
- Visual regression testing for UI components

## Security Considerations

### Authentication Security
- Supabase handles password hashing and session management
- JWT tokens with expiration
- Password reset requires email verification

### Data Security
- Row Level Security (RLS) on all database tables
- Policies based on user role and company membership
- Sensitive data encrypted at rest

### Application Security
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- XSS protection via React's built-in sanitization
- CSRF protection through Supabase's security measures
- Secure headers configured in production

## Performance Optimizations

### Frontend Performance
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- React Query caching reduces API calls
- Virtual scrolling for large lists

### Database Performance
- Indexed queries on frequently accessed columns
- Efficient RLS policies to avoid complex joins
- Connection pooling in Supabase
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates

### Build Performance
- Vite's fast compilation and bundling
- SWC for faster TypeScript compilation
- Tree shaking removes unused code
- Asset optimization and compression

## Future Considerations

### Scalability
- Database query optimization for large datasets
- CDN integration for global performance
- Microservices architecture for complex features
- Caching strategies for improved response times

### Feature Extensions
- Mobile app development
- API integrations with third-party services
- Advanced reporting and analytics
- Workflow automation and notifications
- Multi-language support

This technical context provides a comprehensive understanding of the Beudox HR codebase, enabling effective development, debugging, and feature implementation.
